Vue Custom Components Lost Their Highlighting in VSCode — Here's the Fix
Vue custom components lost their highlighting in VSCode
Recently noticed Volar's custom-component highlighting wasn't behaving, so I'm jotting down the cause.
Normally in Vue, custom components show up with green highlighting — really handy because you can immediately tell which tags are your own components. Like this:
But after some VSCode update, everything looks like this:
So let me record what happened.
My first instinct was to check Vue's
extension — figured the extension had a bug. I uninstalled and reinstalled, no luck. Then I found this issue:
Syntax highlighting seems different from the previous version
OK so someone else hit the same thing — but the official fix landed a year ago. I checked my extension version, and it was already the latest. So not it. Sad face.
Next I started suspecting VSCode itself had quietly changed or added a setting in some update. Poked around VSCode settings and found:
language-json shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
This setting controls semantic highlighting for supported languages — and for some reason mine was set to
fixed everything (or just delete the line, since
If this didn't get fixed, it'd genuinely make dev and maintenance way harder — we're visual creatures and colour matters.

html pre.shiki code .s8XSl, html code.shiki .s8XSl{--shiki-light:#24292E;--shiki-default:#24292E;--shiki-dark:#F0F3F6}html pre.shiki code .sGBqm, html code.shiki .sGBqm{--shiki-light:#6A737D;--shiki-default:#6A737D;--shiki-dark:#BDC4CC}html pre.shiki code .sWTrB, html code.shiki .sWTrB{--shiki-light:#005CC5;--shiki-default:#005CC5;--shiki-dark:#72F088}html pre.shiki code .stw71, html code.shiki .stw71{--shiki-light:#005CC5;--shiki-default:#005CC5;--shiki-dark:#91CBFF}html .light .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-light);background: var(--shiki-light-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-light-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-light-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-light-text-decoration);}html.light .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-light);background: var(--shiki-light-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-light-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-light-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-light-text-decoration);}html .default .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}html.dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}
Noticed Volar's custom-component highlighting wasn't working right, so I dug into it and found the cause — sharing the fix in case you hit the same thing.
